definition: Z = ρc (Pa·s/m = Rayl); specific acoustic impedance of a medium; mismatch drives reflections
Layer inserted between transducer and medium to reduce mismatch; quarter-wave (λ/4) thickness gives maximum transmission at center frequency
Ultrasonic measurements without liquid couplant; main challenge is PZT-to-air impedance mismatch (~33 MRayl vs 0.000429 MRayl → 90 dB loss)
Theory of elastic wave propagation in fluid-saturated porous media; predicts two P-waves and one S-wave; transition frequency ωc = ηφ/(ρ₀κ); correction by Kurzeja-Steeb 2012
ML impedance selection: Z_opt = √(Z_PZT × Z_air) for single layer; geometric mean; gives widest bandwidth but lowest peak
Dynamic tortuosity α(ω) and bulk modulus K(ω) for rigid-frame porous media; extends Biot to high-frequency rigid limit; two parameters (tortuosity α∞, viscous length Λ)
Capacitive thin-membrane transducer; requires DC bias; high sensitivity; CMOS-compatible
Dimensionless measure of electromechanical conversion efficiency; k²_t (thickness mode); k²_31 (lateral mode); k²_eff from impedance
PVDF film corrugated to amplify flexural motion; resonance f₀ = (1/2πR)√(Y/ρp); used for air-coupled ranging at 200 kHz
Binomial ML impedance: Z₁ = (Z_PZT² × Z_air)^(1/3); gives highest peak transmission at f₀ but narrowest band
Voice-coil driven metal cap transducer; no PZT; suited for <40 kHz air-coupled applications
Metal cap with PZT disc; resonance set by cap geometry: f = (λ/a)²√(D/ρh); d31 coupling; no matching layer needed
Evolutionary algorithm (roulette-wheel + crossover + mutation) applied to select ML material sequences from material database to maximise transmission coefficient T
λ/2 resonator formed by SR+MF composite; SR (0.94 MRayl) + MSR (0.54 MRayl) + SF + MF (0.12 MRayl); achieves impedance gradient without needing Z_opt material
Epoxy filled with glass microspheres; ρ = 665 kg/m³, c = 2862 m/s, Z = 1.91 MRayl at 700 kHz; first ML in two-layer design
Krimholtz-Leedom-Matthaei (1970) transmission-line equivalent circuit for thickness-mode piezoelectric transducer; supersedes Mason circuit for multi-layer analysis
Structure with subwavelength resonating inclusions; negative effective ρ or K near resonance → band gap; first demonstrated Liu et al. 2000 (rubber-coated spheres)
Z = 0.24 MRayl, c = 800 m/s, ρ = 300 kg/m³; used as outer ML in air transducers; α = 3 dB/mm
Array of PZT pillars in polymer matrix; reduced Z (3–12 MRayl) and lower Qm; improved efficiency for air-coupled applications
MEMS-based bending-mode transducer; thin PZT or AlN on Si membrane; d31 coupling; applications: IVUS, rangefinder, fingerprint
Silicone rubber + porosity φ: cL drops from 1110 → 38.5 m/s at φ = 35%; K₀/G₀ ≈ 4000 → dramatic impedance reduction
kt = 0.3, ρ = 1.89 g/cm³, c = 2400 m/s, Z ≈ 4.5 MRayl; lower Qm than PZT; used for air-coupled arrays at 2 MHz
Flexible piezoelectric polymer; d31 = 20–30 pC/N, Z ≈ 2.7 MRayl; used for hydrophones, air transducers, wideband probes
P(VDF-TrFE) absorbs ~5 wt% water over 4 weeks; reversible; Young's modulus +9.33%; d₁₁₀ lattice spacing increases
ML with thickness = λ/4 at center frequency; Z_input = Z_ML²/Z_load; for perfect matching Z_ML = √(Z₁Z₂)
Thin polymer film at precise air gap from transducer; impedance conversion Z_in ≈ (4/π)Z_m·Q_m; ~10 dB pressure gain
Closed-cell polymer foam with Es = 5 GPa; Z = 0.05 MRayl, v > 640 m/s, α < 100 Np/m at 1 MHz; preferred over open-cell (lower α, thicker layer, larger pore tolerance)
c_mix = (1-φ)c_solid + φ·c_fluid; empirical additive formula for porous media
Z = 0.01–0.1 MRayl; c = 120 m/s; fragile; first used by Khuri-Yakub 1988; Airloy X103L is machinable cross-linked variant
Polymer-metal bilayer: ZIN = ZM²/Z_R (equivalent to λ/4 layer of metal); enhances radiation for air-coupled PZT
PVDF multilayer off-resonance wideband design; Barker-code weighted layers; –6 dB BW 7.8 MHz; coded excitation and pulse compression
Z_opt = (2·Z_PZT·Z_air²)^(1/3); quality-factor equalization criterion; intermediate between Chebyshev and DeSilets
Parameter α∞ ≥ 1 characterising tortuous path length through porous medium; appears in Biot and Champoux-Allard models
Fourpole cascade matrices for multilayer acoustic/electrical systems; equivalent to KLM for thickness-mode stacks
DLP-based additive manufacturing of matching layers from photocurable resin + glass micro-balloons; 28% porosity; v = 1865 m/s at 200 kHz
Parallel LCR (mechanical branch) + capacitor C₀ (static); simple model for single-resonance piezoelectric; Q_m from LCR parameters
